papers.title: Trust Region On-Policy Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract Trust Region On-Policy Distillation (TrOPD) improves reliable token-level supervision in large language model distillation by using trust regions, outlier estimation, and off-policy guidance to address instability issues under distribution mismatch.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct On-Policy Distillation (OPD) is a fundamental technique for efficient post-training of large language models (LLMs), with broad applications in agent learning, multi-task enhancement, and model compression. However, OPD training becomes unstable when the teacher and student distributions differ substantially, as teacher supervision on student-generated tokens may yield unreliable policy gradients and even cause optimization failure. This work addresses reliable on-policy token-level supervision through credit assignment strategies, and proposes Trust Region On-Policy Distillation, TrOPD. It features the following characteristics: 1) Trust-Region On-Policy Learning: TrOPD performs OPD only in regions where the teacher provides reliable supervision, mitigating the optimization difficulty of the K1 reverse-KL estimator under distribution mismatch. 2) Outlier Estimation: For outlier regions, we explore gradient clipping, masking, and forward-KL estimation to reduce the adverse effects of unreliable supervision. 3) Off-Policy Guidance: The student continues generation from teacher prefixes and uses forward KL to imitate off-policy guidance, encouraging on-policy exploration toward reliable regions. Experiments show that TrOPD consistently outperforms SoTA OPD baselines, including OPD, EOPD, and REOPOLD, across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and general-domain benchmarks.

ru.title: Надёжная дистилляция через доверительные регионы и умное управление выбросами
ru.desc: В статье представлен метод Trust Region On-Policy Distillation (TrOPD) для стабилизации дистилляции больших языковых моделей. Основная проблема заключается в нестабильности обучения when распределения учителя и студента существенно различаются, что приводит к ненадёжным градиентам политики. Авторы предлагают три ключевых компонента: обучение в доверительных областях для надёжного обучения, обработка выбросов через clipping и masking, а также off-policy guidance для исследования надёжных регионов. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что TrOPD превосходит существующие методы on-policy distillation на задачах математического рассуждения, генерации кода и других бенчмарках.

en.title: Enhancing Distillation Stability with Trust Regions
en.desc: The paper introduces Trust Region On-Policy Distillation (TrOPD), a method designed to enhance the reliability of token-level supervision in the distillation of large language models (LLMs). It addresses the instability that arises when the distributions of teacher and student models differ significantly, which can lead to unreliable policy gradients. TrOPD employs strategies such as trust-region learning to ensure that training occurs only in areas where the teacher's supervision is dependable, and it uses outlier estimation techniques to manage the impact of unreliable data. The method demonstrates superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art on-policy distillation techniques across various tasks, including mathematical reasoning and code generation.

zh.title: 信任区域提升在线蒸馏的可靠性
zh.desc: 信任区域在线蒸馏（TrOPD）通过使用信任区域、异常值估计和离线指导，改善了大语言模型蒸馏中的可靠性问题。在线蒸馏（OPD）是一种高效的后训练技术，但当教师和学生的分布差异较大时，训练会变得不稳定。TrOPD 通过信用分配策略解决了在线令牌级监督的可靠性问题，确保在教师提供可靠监督的区域内进行优化。实验表明，TrOPD 在数学推理、代码生成和通用基准测试中，始终优于现有的 OPD 基线。

papers.title: Humanoid-GPT: Scaling Data and Structure for Zero-Shot Motion Tracking
papers.abstract: Abstract Humanoid-GPT is a GPT-style Transformer with causal attention trained on a billion-scale motion corpus that achieves zero-shot generalization to unseen motions and control tasks through scalable pre-training on diverse motion data.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce Humanoid-GPT, a GPT-style Transformer with causal attention trained on a billion-scale motion corpus for whole-body control. Unlike prior shallow MLP trackers constrained by scarce data and an agility-generalization trade-off, Humanoid-GPT is pre-trained on a 2B-frame retargeted corpus that unifies all major mocap datasets with large-scale in-house recordings. Scaling both data and model capacity yields a single generative Transformer that tracks highly dynamic behaviors while achieving unprecedented zero-shot generalization to unseen motions and control tasks. Extensive experiments and scaling analyses show that our model establishes a new performance frontier, demonstrating robust zero-shot generalization to unseen tasks while simultaneously tracking highly dynamic and complex motions.

ru.title: Масштабный трансформер для реалистичного управления движениями человекоида
ru.desc: Humanoid-GPT — это трансформер в стиле GPT с причинной аттентацией, обученный на корпусе из миллиарда кадров движений для управления полным телом человекоида. В отличие от предыдущих моделей, основанных на МЛП с ограниченными данными, эта модель предварительно обучена на 2 млрд кадров, объединяющих все основные датасеты захвата движений с большим объёмом внутренних записей. Масштабирование как данных, так и архитектуры модели позволило создать единый генеративный трансформер, который отслеживает сложные динамические движения и достигает нулевой обобщаемости на неиспользованные в обучении движения и задачи управления. Экспериментальные результаты и анализ масштабирования демонстрируют новый уровень производительности с надёжной нулевой обобщаемостью при одновременном отслеживании высокодинамичных и сложных движений.

en.title: Revolutionizing Motion Control with Zero-Shot Generalization
en.desc: Humanoid-GPT is a novel GPT-style Transformer designed for whole-body motion control, utilizing causal attention mechanisms. It is trained on a massive dataset comprising a billion motion frames, allowing it to generalize to new and unseen motion tasks without prior specific training. This model overcomes limitations of previous shallow MLP trackers by leveraging a diverse and extensive motion corpus, which includes major motion capture datasets and in-house recordings. The results indicate that Humanoid-GPT achieves superior performance in tracking complex behaviors and demonstrates remarkable zero-shot generalization capabilities.

zh.title: Humanoid-GPT：突破性的人形控制模型
zh.desc: Humanoid-GPT是一种基于GPT的变换器，采用因果注意力机制，训练于十亿规模的运动数据集。与之前受限于数据稀缺的浅层多层感知器不同，Humanoid-GPT通过在一个包含2B帧的重定向数据集上进行预训练，能够实现对复杂动态行为的跟踪。该模型在未见过的运动和控制任务上实现了零-shot泛化，展现出强大的适应能力。实验结果表明，Humanoid-GPT在性能上达到了新的前沿，能够同时处理复杂的动态动作。

papers.title: A Local Perturbation Theory for Cross-Domain Interference and Recovery in Multi-Domain RL
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-domain reinforcement learning in language models causes performance degradation through shared computational pathways, but targeted refresh and rollback techniques can selectively recover lost capabilities with minimal side effects.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reinforcement learning (RL) post-training improves large language models (LLMs) on individual domains such as mathematical reasoning, code generation, question answering, and creative writing (CW), but training on one domain often degrades performance on others. Existing explanations based on catastrophic forgetting or global gradient conflict are incomplete: substantial interference can occur even when full-model gradients are nearly orthogonal. We show that single-domain RL produces sparse, small-magnitude parameter edits with weak overlap among top-changed neurons, while different domains still share substantial active computation routes on which update directions determine whether they act synergistically or conflict. Guided by this observation, we prove under a local perturbation model of multi-domain RL that later-domain training harms an earlier domain mainly through a second-order damage term, which under the observed sparse route structure concentrates in a low-dimensional shared conflict subspace. Moreover, a short domain refresh contracts the harmful component on this subspace, enabling selective recovery with limited collateral damage. Consistent with the theory, a brief Re-Math refresh after Code rightarrow Math rightarrow QA rightarrow CW recovers Math from 57.66 to 66.04 while largely preserving performance on the other domains, yielding the best average score of 66.39. Beyond refresh, a training-free rollback on a sparse proxy conflict coordinate set for the Math-QA pair partially restores Math, providing direct proxy-level evidence for localized damage. These results provide a localized mechanistic account of interference and recovery in multi-domain RL.
affiliations: TJUNLP Lab, College of Intelligence and Computing, Tianjin University, Tianjin, China

ru.title: Локализованное восстановление способностей в многодоменном обучении с подкреплением
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема деградации производительности больших языковых моделей при обучении с подкреплением на нескольких доменах одновременно. Авторы показали, что интерференция между доменами происходит не из-за глобального конфликта градиентов, а через локализованные общие вычислительные пути, где обновления параметров вызывают второго порядка повреждения. Предложены два метода восстановления потерянных способностей: краткое переобучение на проблемном домене (refresh) и обучение-свободный откат на разреженном наборе конфликтных координат (rollback). Экспериментальные результаты подтверждают теорию и демонстрируют эффективное восстановление производительности с минимальным влиянием на другие домены.

en.title: Recovering Lost Skills in Multi-Domain Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: This paper discusses how training language models using reinforcement learning (RL) on multiple domains can lead to performance issues due to shared computational pathways. It identifies that while RL can enhance performance in specific areas like math or coding, it can also degrade capabilities in other domains due to interference. The authors propose techniques like targeted refresh and rollback to recover lost skills with minimal negative impact on other areas. Their findings suggest that understanding the structure of shared computation can help mitigate the adverse effects of multi-domain training.

zh.title: 多领域强化学习中的干扰与恢复
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了多领域强化学习在语言模型中的性能下降问题，主要是由于共享的计算路径导致的干扰。研究表明，单领域的强化学习会对模型参数进行稀疏的小幅度编辑，而不同领域之间仍然共享大量的计算路径。通过引入目标刷新和回滚技术，研究者能够选择性地恢复丢失的能力，同时对其他领域的影响最小。实验结果显示，短时间的领域刷新可以有效减少干扰，恢复模型在特定领域的性能。

papers.title: AutoMedBench: Towards Medical AutoResearch with Agentic AI Models
papers.abstract: Abstract AutoMedBench presents a comprehensive benchmark for autonomous medical-AI research that evaluates agent performance across five workflow stages, revealing validation as the weakest stage and highlighting the importance of reliable pipeline execution and verification in medical AI workflows.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Autonomous agents are increasingly expected to support end-to-end medical-AI research workflows, moving beyond isolated prediction tasks or short-form clinical question answering. However, existing medical agent benchmarks primarily evaluate final outputs, providing limited visibility into agent behavior within the research process. To address this gap, we present AutoMedBench, a workflow-aware benchmark for autonomous medical-AI research across diverse medical imaging and multimodal inference tasks, organizing agent execution into a unified five-stage workflow (S1-S5): Plan, Setup, Validate, Inference, and Submit. It comprises long-horizon tasks with each run averaging 33 agent turns, spanning five research tracks: segmentation, image enhancement, visual question answering (VQA), report generation, and lesion detection. Each task is evaluated under two difficulty tiers, Lite and Standard, which use the same data and metrics but differ in the amount of task-brief scaffolding, and each run is scored using both final task performance and S1-S5 stage scores, enabling stage-level analysis from the initial task brief to the final submitted artifact. Across thousands of recorded runs, stage-level scoring reveals that Validate is the weakest workflow stage on average, whereas Setup is the strongest, suggesting that current agents are better at making pipelines executable than at verifying their reliability. Post-run error analysis further shows that verification and submission failures dominate tagged errors, accounting for 37.7% and 38.1% of fired codes respectively, whereas task-understanding errors are rare at 0.9%, and runs with one fired error code have a 48% lower overall score than runs with no error code on average.

ru.title: От планирования к верификации: где ломаются медицинские AI агенты
ru.desc: AutoMedBench представляет комплексный бенчмарк для оценки автономных агентов в медицинских AI исследованиях, разбивая рабочий процесс на пять этапов: планирование, подготовка, валидация, вывод и отправка результатов. Исследование включает долгосрочные задачи с множеством ходов агента, охватывающие сегментацию изображений, улучшение качества, ответы на визуальные вопросы, генерацию отчетов и обнаружение поражений. Анализ показал, что валидация является самым слабым этапом рабочего процесса, в то время как подготовка пайплайна выполняется наиболее успешно, что указывает на недостаток в проверке надежности создаваемых систем. Результаты демонстрируют, что основные ошибки связаны с верификацией и отправкой результатов, подчеркивая важность надежного выполнения и валидации в медицинских AI рабочих процессах.

en.title: Enhancing Medical AI: A Five-Stage Benchmark for Autonomous Agents
en.desc: AutoMedBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate the performance of autonomous agents in medical AI research across five key workflow stages: Plan, Setup, Validate, Inference, and Submit. It highlights that the validation stage is the weakest point in the workflow, indicating a need for better reliability in medical AI systems. The benchmark assesses tasks in areas like segmentation and visual question answering, providing insights into agent behavior throughout the research process rather than just final outputs. By scoring both the overall task performance and individual stage performance, AutoMedBench allows for a detailed analysis of how well agents execute complex medical workflows.

zh.title: 提升医疗AI工作流程的验证能力
zh.desc: AutoMedBench是一个全面的基准测试，旨在评估自主医疗人工智能在五个工作流程阶段的表现。研究发现，验证阶段是最薄弱的环节，强调了在医疗AI工作流程中可靠的管道执行和验证的重要性。该基准测试涵盖了多种医学成像和多模态推理任务，组织了一个统一的五阶段工作流程。通过对数千次运行的分析，结果显示，当前的代理在执行管道方面表现较好，但在验证其可靠性方面仍有待提高。

papers.title: World Models Meet Language Models: On the Complementarity of Concrete and Abstract Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Controlled concrete reasoning combines visual simulation with abstract reasoning through a training method that uses privileged future information to improve prediction accuracy and robustness.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct World models and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) provide complementary capabilities for predicting future outcomes from static visual observations. World models can generate concrete visual rollouts of possible futures, while MLLMs can reason abstractly over questions, goals, and rules. However, generated rollouts are stochastic and may be visually plausible but task-incorrect, making it necessary to determine when visual simulation is useful, whether a rollout is credible, and how it should influence the final answer. We formulate this problem as controlled concrete reasoning, where a model learns to invoke, verify, and integrate visual future simulation alongside abstract reasoning. To study this setting, we construct two human-verified benchmarks, VRQABench for controllable spatial lookahead and OpenWorldQA for open-domain physical prediction, and propose Privileged-Future On-Policy Self-Distillation (PF-OPSD). During training, PF-OPSD uses ground-truth future videos and answers only as teacher-side privileged context to evaluate on-policy concrete-reasoning trajectories, while the deployable student never observes true futures at test time. Experimental results show that PF-OPSD outperforms baseline by 10.6% and 10.9% on VRQABench and OpenWorldQA, respectively, while increasing robustness to noisy or conflicting rollouts. Our code and dataset are available at https://github.com/yczhou001/PF-OPSD.

ru.title: Синтез визуальной симуляции и логического мышления через привилегированное обучение
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод управляемого конкретного рассуждения, который комбинирует визуальную симуляцию с абстрактным логическим мышлением для улучшения предсказания будущих событий. Авторы используют привилегированную информацию о будущем на этапе обучения через метод самодистилляции PF-OPSD, где учитель получает доступ к истинным видео и ответам, а ученик учится предсказывать без такой информации. Предложены два benchmark'а (VRQABench и OpenWorldQA) с человеческой верификацией для оценки пространственного планирования и физических предсказаний. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшение на 10.6-10.9% и повышение устойчивости к шумным или конфликтующим симуляциям.

en.title: Enhancing Predictions with Controlled Concrete Reasoning
en.desc: This paper introduces controlled concrete reasoning, which enhances prediction accuracy by combining visual simulation with abstract reasoning. It leverages world models to generate potential future scenarios and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) for abstract reasoning about tasks and goals. The authors propose a training method called Privileged-Future On-Policy Self-Distillation (PF-OPSD) that uses future information to guide the learning process without exposing the model to true future data during testing. Experimental results demonstrate that PF-OPSD significantly improves performance on two benchmarks while also increasing robustness against misleading visual simulations.

zh.title: 控制性具体推理：结合视觉与抽象的智能预测
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种控制性具体推理的方法，将视觉模拟与抽象推理结合起来。通过使用特权未来信息的训练方法，提升了预测的准确性和鲁棒性。研究中构建了两个基准测试，分别用于可控空间前瞻和开放领域物理预测，并提出了特权未来在线自蒸馏（PF-OPSD）方法。实验结果表明，PF-OPSD在这两个基准上均优于基线，且对噪声或冲突的结果具有更强的鲁棒性。

papers.title: TRON: Targeted Rule-Verifiable Online Environments for Visual Reasoning RL
papers.abstract: Abstract TRON enables scalable and controllable reinforcement learning for visual reasoning through an online environment substrate that generates unlimited diverse training instances with verifiable answers.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reinforcement learning (RL) for visual reasoning needs scalable, verifiable, and controllable training signals. Existing visual RL post-training trains on static curated datasets, with fixed image-question-answer samples bounded by their collection budget. In this work, we introduce TRON (Targeted, Rule-verifiable Online eNvironments), an online environment substrate: a training rollout is generated on demand by a controllable generator-verifier program that samples a fresh latent visual state, renders an image, asks a question, and exactly verifies the answer. A single run can therefore draw an unbounded stream of fresh instances at the difficulty level required by the current curriculum. The current TRON suite contains 520 environments organized into five ability buckets (spatial, mathematical, diagram, pattern/logic, and counting); the same substrate supports both a single full model trained on all buckets and per-bucket ability-specialist models, with no additional data collection. We also introduce a substrate analysis covering generation reliability, instance and level diversity, cross-environment near-duplicates, and base-model pass rate by difficulty level. RL post-training with METHOD consistently improves performance on ten external multimodal reasoning benchmarks across Qwen3-VL-4B, Qwen2.5-VL-7B, and MiMo-VL-7B-SFT.

ru.title: Бесконечные верифицируемые окружения для обучения визуального рассуждения
ru.desc: TRON представляет собой онлайн-среду для обучения с подкреплением визуальных моделей рассуждения, которая генерирует неограниченное количество разнообразных тренировочных примеров с верифицируемыми ответами. В отличие от существующих подходов, использующих статические наборы данных, TRON динамически создаёт задачи на лету через управляемую программу генератора-верификатора, которая может адаптироваться к уровню сложности в процессе обучения. Набор содержит 520 окружений, организованных по пяти категориям способностей (пространственное мышление, математика, диаграммы, логика и счёт), позволяя обучать как универсальные, так и специализированные модели. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют улучшение производительности на десяти внешних мультимодальных бенчмарках благодаря RL post-training.

en.title: TRON: Unlimited Training for Visual Reasoning in RL
en.desc: TRON is a novel framework that enhances reinforcement learning (RL) for visual reasoning by providing an online environment that generates diverse training instances with guaranteed correct answers. Unlike traditional methods that rely on static datasets, TRON creates training scenarios on demand, allowing for scalable and controllable learning experiences. It features a generator-verifier system that produces fresh visual states, images, and questions, ensuring that the training material is tailored to the learner's current skill level. The TRON suite includes 520 environments categorized by different reasoning abilities, enabling both comprehensive and specialized training without the need for additional data collection.

zh.title: TRON：无限生成的视觉推理训练环境
zh.desc: TRON是一种增强学习框架，旨在通过在线环境生成多样化的训练实例来提升视觉推理能力。它能够实时生成图像、问题和可验证的答案，从而提供可控的训练信号。与传统的静态数据集不同，TRON可以根据当前的学习需求生成无限的训练样本。该系统包含520个环境，支持不同能力的模型训练，显著提高了在多模态推理基准上的表现。

papers.title: Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion Models for Unified and Data Efficient Image-to-Image Translation
papers.abstract: Abstract Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion models (DRDD) improve unified image-to-image translation by separating noise diffusion for domain harmonization from residual diffusion for semantic mapping, enhancing data efficiency and performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We propose Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion models (DRDD) for unified and data-efficient image-to-image (I2I) translation. While diffusion models have advanced I2I translation in terms of quality and diversity, we uncover a previously under-explored property in diffusion models. Crucially, beyond its conventional role of manifold lifting (i.e., moving data off low-dimensional manifolds), injecting Gaussian noise facilitates domain harmonization by implicitly aligning feature distributions across domains, a property particularly advantageous for unified I2I translation. However, existing diffusion models prematurely erode this harmonization effect, as noise and residuals are simultaneously removed in a single coupled diffusion process. To address this, DRDD decouples the diffusion process into two sequential and independent diffusion stages: (1) a stochastic noise diffusion for domain harmonization and manifold lifting, and (2) a deterministic residual diffusion that learns the core semantic mapping entirely within the fixed-noise domain. This decoupling preserves harmonization and manifold lifting effects throughout the transformation, substantially simplifying the learning of unified mappings across diverse tasks and domains. Notably, the noise diffusion stage is trained exclusively on abundant, unpaired target-domain images, greatly improving data efficiency. Comprehensive theoretical and empirical analysis demonstrates that DRDD is broadly compatible with mainstream diffusion models and consistently delivers robust, unified I2I translation, even under limited paired data. Our code is available at https://github.com/HKU-HealthAI/DRDD.
affiliations: Shenyang Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Развязанная диффузия: разделение гармонизации и трансформации для универсального перевода изображений
ru.desc: В работе предложены Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion models (DRDD) для задачи преобразования изображений между доменами. Авторы показали, что добавление гауссовского шума в диффузионные модели помогает гармонизировать распределения признаков между доменами, но существующие модели теряют этот эффект. Решение заключается в разделении процесса диффузии на два этапа: стохастическую диффузию шума для гармонизации доменов и детерминированную диффузию остатков для семантического отображения. Такое разделение улучшает эффективность обучения на ограниченных парных данных и обеспечивает единообразное преобразование изображений между различными задачами.

en.title: Decoupling for Enhanced Image Translation Efficiency
en.desc: The paper introduces Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion models (DRDD) to enhance image-to-image translation by separating the processes of noise diffusion and residual diffusion. This separation allows for better domain harmonization and semantic mapping, improving the efficiency and performance of the model. By decoupling these processes, DRDD maintains the benefits of noise diffusion while learning the essential mappings in a fixed-noise environment. The approach is shown to be effective even with limited paired data, making it a significant advancement in the field of diffusion models for image translation.

zh.title: 解耦残差去噪扩散模型：提升图像翻译效率
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的模型，称为解耦残差去噪扩散模型（DRDD），用于统一和高效的数据图像到图像（I2I）翻译。该模型通过将噪声扩散和残差扩散分开，增强了数据的利用效率和性能。DRDD的第一阶段进行随机噪声扩散，以实现领域的协调，而第二阶段则进行确定性残差扩散，专注于语义映射。通过这种解耦，DRDD在不同任务和领域之间的统一映射学习变得更加简单，同时保持了领域协调的效果。

papers.title: Ψ-Bench: Evaluating Persona-Sensitive Influencing in Persuasive Dialogues
papers.abstract: Abstract LLMs demonstrate limited effectiveness in persuasive conversation despite generating coherent arguments, with user-specific profiles significantly improving performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Personalization is a crucial capability of modern language agents. However, current research primarily positions personalized agents as passive responders to user preferences, limiting their ability to interact with users and provide suggestions or guidance proactively. To systematically evaluate such proactive personalization in realistic interactions, we propose Ψ-Bench, a benchmark for assessing LLMs' ability to influence realistic users through conversation. We design three real-world interaction scenarios that involve persuasion in Ψ-Bench, and endow simulated clients with personal characteristics through explicit user profiles derived from dialogue histories. We evaluate 10 frontier LLMs on Ψ-Bench and find that while most models can produce coherent and reasonable arguments, even state-of-the-art models still leave considerable room for improvement in persuasion. We also find that providing access to client profiles yields an average performance gain of 18.24\%, highlighting the importance of user-specific information for effective persuasion. Overall, our work highlights persona-sensitive influencing as a challenging yet practical direction for evaluating and developing more proactive personalized LLM agents. Codes are available at: https://github.com/Hanpx20/Psi-Bench.

ru.title: Персонализированное убеждение: когда LLM должны влиять, а не просто отвечать
ru.desc: В этой статье исследуется способность больших языковых моделей к убедительному общению с пользователями в реалистичных сценариях. Авторы предлагают Ψ-Bench, бенчмарк для оценки проактивной персонализации LLM, где модели должны влиять на поведение пользователей через диалог. Результаты показывают, что хотя современные модели генерируют связные аргументы, они значительно отстают в эффективности убеждения, а доступ к профилям пользователей улучшает качество на 18.24%. Исследование подчеркивает важность учета индивидуальных характеристик пользователя для разработки более продвинутых персонализированных языковых агентов.

en.title: Enhancing Persuasion in LLMs through User Personalization
en.desc: This paper discusses the limitations of large language models (LLMs) in engaging users persuasively, despite their ability to generate coherent arguments. It introduces Ψ-Bench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate how well LLMs can influence users through personalized conversations. The study shows that incorporating user-specific profiles significantly enhances the models' persuasive capabilities, with an average performance improvement of 18.24%. The findings suggest that proactive personalization, where models actively guide and suggest to users, is a crucial area for future research in developing more effective conversational agents.

zh.title: 个性化影响力：提升对话模型的说服能力
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在说服性对话中的局限性，尽管它们能够生成连贯的论点。研究表明，用户特定的个人资料显著提高了模型的表现。我们提出了Ψ-Bench，一个用于评估LLMs在真实对话中影响用户能力的基准，设计了三种涉及说服的真实互动场景。结果显示，尽管大多数模型能够生成合理的论点，但在说服能力上仍有很大的提升空间，强调了个性化信息在有效说服中的重要性。

papers.title: Decentralized Instruction Tuning: Conflict-Aware Splitting and Weight Merging
papers.abstract: Abstract Instruction tuning of large language models can be improved through decentralized training that partitions mixed datasets based on gradient conflicts and merges results via weighted averaging, achieving performance comparable to centralized methods with reduced communication overhead.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Instruction tuning aligns large language models, including multimodal ones, with diverse user intents, but scaling to heterogeneous mixtures is hindered by gradient interference and bandwidth-heavy synchronization. We ask whether these two bottlenecks can be addressed jointly by training parts of the mixture independently and reconciling them once in parameter space. We develop a local quadratic theory inside a shared flat basin that yields three results: weight merging produces a curvature-weighted variance reduction; PCA-aligned conflict splitting maximizes this gain along high-curvature directions; and merging additionally acts as spectral filtering with implicit norm regularization. These results directly motivate MERIT, a decentralized merge-ready instruction-tuning pipeline that estimates dataset-level gradient conflicts, partitions the mixture along the top PCA conflict axes, fine-tunes each partition independently with no inter-partition communication, and merges once via token-weighted averaging. On Qwen2.5-VL-3B with 136 Vision-FLAN tasks, MERIT improves the 8-benchmark average from 54.3 (joint training) to 57.0. The same recipe scales to a 7B model on a 1.6M-example, 176-source mixture -- matching or exceeding centralized joint training with minimal cost overhead -- and transfers to text-only FLAN. Our code is available at https://github.com/naver-ai/merit.

ru.title: Децентрализованная настройка через умное слияние моделей
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод MERIT для улучшения инструктивной настройки больших языковых моделей через децентрализованное обучение. Авторы разбивают смешанные датасеты на части, основываясь на конфликтах градиентов, а затем объединяют результаты с помощью взвешенного усреднения параметров. Теоретический анализ показывает, что слияние моделей обеспечивает редукцию дисперсии с учетом кривизны потерь и действует как спектральный фильтр. Экспериментально метод достигает качества централизованного обучения с существенно меньшей коммуникационной нагрузкой на мультимодальных моделях.

en.title: Decentralized Training for Enhanced Instruction Tuning
en.desc: This paper presents a method called MERIT for improving instruction tuning of large language models through decentralized training. It addresses issues of gradient conflicts and communication overhead by partitioning mixed datasets and training them independently before merging results. The approach utilizes a local quadratic theory to optimize weight merging and conflict splitting, enhancing performance while reducing the need for heavy synchronization. The results show that MERIT can achieve better or comparable performance to centralized methods with significantly lower costs, demonstrating its effectiveness across various model sizes and tasks.

zh.title: 去中心化的指令调优新方法
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种名为MERIT的去中心化指令调优方法，旨在提高大型语言模型的训练效率。通过将混合数据集根据梯度冲突进行分区，并在参数空间中合并结果，MERIT能够减少通信开销，同时保持与集中式方法相当的性能。研究表明，局部二次理论可以有效地减少权重合并后的曲率加权方差，并通过主成分分析（PCA）优化冲突分割。最终，MERIT在多个任务上实现了显著的性能提升，证明了其在处理异构数据集时的有效性。

papers.title: Small RL Controller, Large Language Model: RL-Guided Adaptive Sampling for Test-Time Scaling
papers.abstract: Abstract Adaptive sampling for large language models is formulated as a Markov decision process and optimized using reinforcement learning to balance correctness, latency, and computational cost.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Test-time scaling improves the reasoning performance of large language models but incurs substantial cost in both total computation and latency. Existing adaptive sampling methods partially mitigate this issue by dynamically deciding when to stop sampling, yet they typically rely on heuristic rules or rely on distribution assumptions. In this work, we formulate adaptive sampling as a Markov decision process (MDP). We train a lightweight sampling controller with reinforcement learning (RL) to jointly balance answer correctness, latency, and computation cost. At each round, the controller decides to stop sampling or to acquire additional samples. Our method is lightweight which only relies on statistics of final answers, and can be trained and deployed on CPU. We further show that the resulting framework admits an interpretation as the Lagrangian relaxation of a constrained optimization problem with explicit budget constraints. Experiments against strong baselines such as ASC and ESC show that our method achieves improved trade-offs among answer correctness, sampling rounds, and total samples required.
affiliations: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

ru.title: Обучаемый контроллер для оптимального выбора количества образцов в LLM
ru.desc: В работе предлагается новый подход к адаптивной выборке для больших языковых моделей, сформулированный как процесс принятия решений (MDP). Авторы обучают легковесный контроллер с помощью обучения с подкреплением, который одновременно балансирует три конфликтующих критерия: точность ответов, задержку и вычислительные затраты. На каждом шаге контроллер решает, продолжить ли выборку или остановиться, основываясь только на статистике финальных ответов. Метод допускает интерпретацию как релаксация Лагранжа задачи оптимизации с явными бюджетными ограничениями и показывает улучшенные компромиссы по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Optimizing Sampling with Reinforcement Learning for Language Models
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to adaptive sampling for large language models by treating it as a Markov decision process (MDP). The authors use reinforcement learning (RL) to create a lightweight controller that optimally decides when to stop sampling based on the trade-offs between correctness, latency, and computational cost. Unlike previous methods that depend on heuristics, this approach relies solely on the statistics of the final answers, making it efficient for deployment on CPUs. The results demonstrate that this method outperforms existing baselines in balancing the quality of answers with the resources used during sampling.

zh.title: 自适应采样：平衡正确性与效率的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文将大语言模型的自适应采样视为一个马尔可夫决策过程，并利用强化学习进行优化，以平衡正确性、延迟和计算成本。现有的自适应采样方法通常依赖启发式规则或分布假设，无法有效解决计算和延迟的高成本问题。我们的方法通过训练轻量级的采样控制器，动态决定是否停止采样或获取额外样本，从而实现更好的性能。实验结果表明，我们的方法在答案正确性、采样轮次和所需样本总数之间取得了更优的平衡。

papers.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
papers.abstract: Abstract Deep learning models with sleep and dreaming paradigms enable continual learning through memory consolidation and self-improvement phases.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct The past few decades have witnessed significant advances in the design of machine learning algorithms, from early studies on task-specific shallow models to more general deep Large Language Models (LLMs). Despite showing promising results in tasks that require instant prediction or in-context learning, existing models lack the ability to continually learn and effectively transfer their temporal in-context knowledge to their long-term parameters. Inspired by human learning process, we introduce a ''Sleep'' paradigm that allows the models to continually learn, distill their short-term fragile memories into stable long-term knowledge with replay, and recursively improve themselves with ''Dreaming'' process. In more detail, sleep consists of two stages: (1) Memory Consolidation: an upward distillation process, called Knowledge Seeding, where the memories of a smaller-self are distilled into a larger network to provide more capacity while preserving the knowledge. As a proof of concept, we present a new Generalized Distillation process for {Knowledge Seeding} (i.e., the combination of on-policy distillation with Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based imitation learning); (2) Dreaming: a self-improvement phase, where the model uses RL to generate a curriculum of synthetic data to rehearse new knowledge and refine existing capabilities without human supervision. Our experiments on long-horizon, continual learning, knowledge incorporation, and few-shot generalization tasks support the importance of the sleep stage.

ru.title: Непрерывное обучение через сон и сновидения: консолидация памяти для самосовершенствования моделей
ru.desc: В работе предложена парадигма «Сна» для непрерывного обучения глубоких нейросетей, вдохновленная человеческим процессом обучения. Парадигма включает два этапа: консолидация памяти, где знания из меньшей сети дистиллируются в большую через процесс обобщенной дистилляции и обучение с подкреплением, и фаза «Сновидений», где модель самостоятельно генерирует синтетические данные для улучшения своих способностей. Предложенный подход позволяет моделям эффективно перемещать краткосрочные знания из контекста в долгосрочные параметры и рекурсивно улучшать себя без человеческого надзора. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах непрерывного обучения, долгосрочного планирования и обобщения на небольших наборах данных демонстрируют важность предложенной парадигмы.

en.title: Unlocking Continual Learning Through Sleep and Dreaming in AI
en.desc: This paper presents a novel approach to continual learning in deep learning models by mimicking human sleep and dreaming processes. The proposed 'Sleep' paradigm allows models to consolidate short-term memories into long-term knowledge through a two-stage process: Memory Consolidation and Dreaming. Memory Consolidation involves distilling knowledge from a smaller model into a larger one, enhancing capacity while retaining information. The Dreaming phase enables self-improvement by generating synthetic data for the model to practice and refine its skills autonomously, leading to better performance in various learning tasks.

zh.title: 通过睡眠与梦境实现持续学习的深度学习模型
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种新的深度学习模型，通过引入“睡眠”和“梦境”范式来实现持续学习。模型在“睡眠”阶段进行记忆巩固，将短期记忆转化为长期知识，并在“梦境”阶段自我改进。具体来说，记忆巩固包括知识播种的过程，将小模型的记忆提炼到大模型中，以保留知识并增加容量。实验结果表明，这种睡眠阶段对长期学习和知识整合具有重要意义。

papers.title: PlatonicNav: Unveiling Semantic Correspondence in Navigation with Platonic Topological Maps
papers.abstract: Abstract A training-free framework for embodied navigation that uses a vision-only approach to create semantic maps and ground language goals through blind matching without paired vision-language data.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Embodied visual navigation, where an agent perceives a complex environment and acts to reach a goal from raw sensory input, underpins a wide range of applications such as household service robotics, assistive robotics, and large-scale autonomous exploration. However, recent attempts to unify vision-and-language navigation (VLN) and object goal navigation (ObjNav) remain at the level of architectural fusion, mixed-task training, and large vision-language pretraining, without examining whether independently trained vision and language encoders may already share a common semantic structure. Moreover, even object-centric topological maps still ground language goals through explicit cross-modal supervision such as CLIP or large vision-language models, leaving open whether such grounding is possible from a purely vision-built map. To address these challenges, we extend the Platonic Representation Hypothesis to embodied navigation and recast vision-only ObjNav, cross-modal ObjNav, and VLN as three different interfaces to the same object-centric semantic manifold. We further introduce PlatonicNav, a training-free framework whose Platonic Topological Map fuses geometric and semantic node distances from a self-supervised visual encoder, and grounds language goals via blind matching without any paired vision-language data. Extensive experiments on simulation benchmarks including HM3D-IIN, OVON, and R2R-CE on MP3D, together with deployment on Unitree Go2, demonstrate that PlatonicNav generalizes across tasks, modalities, and embodiments without explicit cross-modal training. Code: https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/PlatonicNav. Website: https://aigeeksgroup.github.io/PlatonicNav.

ru.title: Единый семантический многообразие для навигации без обучения
ru.desc: В работе предлагается PlatonicNav — фреймворк без обучения для навигации мобильного робота, который использует только визуальную информацию для создания семантических топологических карт. Авторы применяют гипотезу платонического представления к задачам воплощённой навигации, показывая, что независимо обученные визуальные и языковые кодеры уже обладают общей семантической структурой. Фреймворк объединяет геометрические и семантические расстояния из самоконтролируемого визуального энкодера и привязывает языковые цели через слепое сопоставление без парных данных видения-языка. Экспериментальная проверка на нескольких бенчмарках и физическом роботе доказывает, что подход обобщается на разные задачи, модальности и воплощения без явного кросс-модального обучения.

en.title: Navigating Without Training: The Power of PlatonicNav
en.desc: This paper presents a novel framework called PlatonicNav for embodied navigation that operates without the need for training on paired vision-language data. It introduces a concept known as the Platonic Topological Map, which combines geometric and semantic information from a visual encoder to create a semantic map. The framework allows for grounding language goals through a process called blind matching, which does not rely on explicit cross-modal supervision. Experimental results show that PlatonicNav effectively generalizes across various tasks and environments, demonstrating its versatility in navigation applications.

zh.title: 无训练的视觉导航新框架
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种无训练的框架，用于实现基于视觉的导航，能够通过盲匹配生成语义地图和语言目标，而无需配对的视觉-语言数据。研究表明，独立训练的视觉和语言编码器可能共享相同的语义结构，从而简化了导航任务。论文中引入了PlatonicNav框架，它通过自监督的视觉编码器融合几何和语义节点距离，并通过盲匹配来确定语言目标。实验结果表明，PlatonicNav在不同任务、模态和应用中具有良好的泛化能力。

papers.title: NVIDIA OmniDreams: Real-Time Generative World Model for Closed-Loop Autonomous Vehicle Simulation
papers.abstract: Abstract OmniDreams, a foundation generative world model trained from the Cosmos diffusion model, enables real-time action-conditioned video generation for autonomous driving policy evaluation in complex, unseen scenarios.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct As autonomous vehicle capabilities advance, the safe evaluation of driving policies in long-tail scenarios remains a critical bottleneck. In closed-loop simulation, the driving policy model actively interacts with the environment, where its actions dynamically update the simulator state and directly influence the next set of generated sensor observations. While recent reconstruction-based neural simulators offer photorealism, they are fundamentally constrained by their initial captured data and struggle to generalize to highly dynamic or novel scenes. To overcome these limitations, we introduce OmniDreams, a foundation generative world model mid- and post-trained from the Cosmos diffusion model to autoregressively generate action-conditioned videos in real time. By leveraging the rich visual priors of Cosmos and mid- and post-training on 21k hours of driving scenarios, OmniDreams synthesizes complex, unobserved phenomena that are hard for traditional simulators to capture, such as extreme weather and unpredictable dynamic agent behaviors. Crucially, it autoregressively conditions its photorealistic sensor generation on past frames, the current simulator state, and immediate driving actions. Deployed in a closed-loop system with the Alpamayo 1 policy model and AlpaSim orchestrator, OmniDreams acts as a highly responsive, reactive environment, providing a scalable and comprehensive solution for training and evaluating next-generation autonomous driving policies. We additionally show preliminary results indicating that a world-action model (WAM) post-trained from OmniDreams achieves strong performance on the Physical AI Autonomous Vehicles NuRec dataset, surpassing the VLA-based Alpamayo 1.5 research policy model while using only 1/5 the total parameters. These results highlight the potential for a real-time world model like OmniDreams to also serve as a backbone for policy architectures.

ru.title: Генеративный мир для обучения автономного вождения в реальном времени
ru.desc: OmniDreams — это обобщённая генеративная модель мира, созданная на основе диффузионной модели Cosmos, которая в реальном времени генерирует видео, обусловленные действиями агента. Модель обучена на 21 тысяче часов сценариев вождения и способна синтезировать сложные явления, которые традиционные симуляторы не могут захватить, такие как экстремальная погода и непредсказуемое поведение динамических объектов. OmniDreams используется в замкнутом контуре управления вместе с моделью политики Alpamayo для обучения и оценки политик автономного вождения в редких и незнакомых сценариях. Модель демонстрирует практическое применение генеративных подходов для создания реалистичных симуляторов, которые превосходят традиционные методы по способности обобщаться на новые ситуации.

en.title: Revolutionizing Autonomous Driving Evaluation with OmniDreams
en.desc: OmniDreams is a generative world model designed for real-time video generation to evaluate autonomous driving policies in complex scenarios. It uses the Cosmos diffusion model to create realistic simulations that adapt to the actions of the driving policy, allowing for dynamic interaction with the environment. Unlike traditional simulators, which are limited by their initial data, OmniDreams can generate novel situations, such as extreme weather and unpredictable behaviors, by leveraging extensive training on diverse driving scenarios. This innovative approach not only enhances the realism of simulations but also improves the training and evaluation of next-generation autonomous driving systems.

zh.title: OmniDreams：实时生成复杂驾驶场景的智能模型
zh.desc: OmniDreams是一种基础生成世界模型，利用Cosmos扩散模型进行训练，能够实时生成基于动作的驾驶视频，以评估复杂场景下的自动驾驶策略。该模型通过与环境的闭环交互，动态更新模拟器状态，从而影响生成的传感器观察。与传统的重建神经模拟器相比，OmniDreams能够合成极端天气和不可预测的动态行为等复杂现象，克服了初始数据的限制。通过在21,000小时的驾驶场景上进行中后期训练，OmniDreams为下一代自动驾驶政策的训练和评估提供了可扩展的解决方案。

papers.title: Benchmarking Visual State Tracking in Multimodal Video Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract Current multimodal large language models struggle with visual state tracking in videos, performing poorly even when human-level capabilities are required, and existing agentic approaches do not effectively address these limitations.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Understanding a video requires more than recognizing isolated moments, as humans continuously track entities, states, and events over time. This capacity for visual state tracking is fundamental to video understanding, yet remains underexplored in current evaluations of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). We introduce Visual STAte Tracking benchmark (VSTAT), a video-based benchmark designed to diagnose visual state tracking in MLLMs. VSTAT consists of 834 clips drawn from both synthetic and real-world videos, paired with 1,500 questions that cannot be answered from any single frame or short segment, requiring continuous perception and integration of events across the entire video stream. Despite their strong performance on existing video benchmarks, we find that state-of-the-art MLLMs perform far below humans and only modestly above answer-prior baselines. To analyze this gap, we compare MLLMs' thinking traces with the underlying video stream to understand why and when MLLMs fail on VSTAT. We find that MLLMs reason and track correctly in text, but fail at visually perceiving the events they need to track. Finally, our preliminary evaluation suggests that recent agentic approaches, including MLLM-based video agents and coding agents, do not readily resolve these failures, still falling short on VSTAT.

ru.title: Диагностика слепоты языковых моделей к визуальному отслеживанию состояния в видео
ru.desc: В работе представлен новый бенчмарк VSTAT для оценки способности мультимодальных больших языковых моделей (MLLM) отслеживать визуальное состояние в видео. Бенчмарк содержит 834 видеоклипа и 1500 вопросов, которые невозможно ответить из одного кадра и требуют непрерывного восприятия событий на протяжении всего видео. Авторы обнаружили, что современные MLLM значительно отстают от человека в этой задаче, при этом их рассуждения в тексте остаются корректными, но визуальное восприятие необходимых событий нарушается. Исследование показывает, что даже агентные подходы, основанные на MLLM и кодировании, не решают выявленные проблемы отслеживания состояния.

en.title: Enhancing Video Understanding: Bridging the Gap in Visual State Tracking
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in visual state tracking within videos. It introduces the Visual STAte Tracking benchmark (VSTAT), which evaluates MLLMs' ability to continuously perceive and integrate information across video clips. The study reveals that while MLLMs perform well on traditional benchmarks, they significantly underperform compared to humans on VSTAT, particularly in visually tracking events. The authors analyze the reasoning processes of MLLMs and find that their failures stem from inadequate visual perception, indicating that current agentic approaches do not effectively improve their performance.

zh.title: 提升视频理解的视觉状态跟踪能力
zh.desc: 当前的多模态大型语言模型在视频中的视觉状态跟踪方面表现不佳，尤其是在需要人类水平能力的情况下。理解视频不仅仅是识别孤立的瞬间，人类能够持续跟踪实体、状态和事件。我们提出了视觉状态跟踪基准（VSTAT），这是一个旨在诊断多模态大型语言模型视觉状态跟踪能力的视频基准。尽管现有模型在其他视频基准上表现良好，但在VSTAT上，它们的表现远低于人类，且仅略高于基线水平。

papers.title: MERIT: Learning Disentangled Music Representations for Audio Similarity
papers.abstract: Abstract MERIT framework learns disentangled music representations for melody, rhythm, and timbre through conditional audio generation and source-separated stems, enabling nuanced musical queries.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Current music similarity models typically compute a single, monolithic score, entangling distinct musical dimensions like melody, rhythm, and timbre. This limits user control and interpretability, making it impossible to execute nuanced queries. We introduce MERIT, a framework for learning disentangled, factor-specific music representations tailored to these three core dimensions. To overcome the lack of isolated musical variations in real-world audio, we use a novel training strategy that uses conditional audio generation and source-separated stems to strongly encourage single-factor variation in training data. Our evaluations demonstrate strong factor-wise disentanglement. Each head responds strongly to its intended perceptual dimension while remaining near chance on the others, a representational property that holds across both the synthetic training domain and independent real-world audio.
affiliations: Singapore University of Technology and Design

ru.title: Разделённые представления музыки для точных музыкальных запросов
ru.desc: В статье представлена система MERIT для обучения разделённых представлений музыки по трём ключевым компонентам: мелодии, ритму и тембру. Авторы предлагают новый метод обучения, использующий условную генерацию аудио и разделённые источники звука, чтобы каждый компонент варьировался независимо. Разработанный подход позволяет моделям лучше разобраться в отдельных музыкальных измерениях и отвечать на более точные и детальные запросы пользователей. Полученные результаты показывают, что каждое представление эффективно реагирует на свой компонент и игнорирует остальные, что подтверждено как на синтетических, так и на реальных аудиоданных.

en.title: Disentangling Music: MERIT for Nuanced Queries
en.desc: The MERIT framework is designed to learn separate music representations for melody, rhythm, and timbre, allowing for more detailed musical queries. Traditional music similarity models often produce a single score that combines these elements, which limits user control and understanding. MERIT addresses this by using conditional audio generation and source-separated stems to ensure that each musical dimension can be varied independently during training. Evaluations show that the framework effectively disentangles these factors, with each component responding specifically to its intended musical aspect while being less sensitive to the others.

zh.title: MERIT：解耦音乐表示的创新框架
zh.desc: MERIT框架通过条件音频生成和源分离音轨学习解耦的音乐表示，专注于旋律、节奏和音色三个核心维度。这种方法克服了传统音乐相似性模型的局限性，使得用户能够进行更细致的音乐查询。通过新颖的训练策略，MERIT能够在训练数据中强烈鼓励单一因素的变化，从而实现更好的解耦效果。评估结果表明，每个因素在其预期的感知维度上表现出强烈的响应，而在其他维度上则接近随机，显示出良好的表示特性。

papers.title: Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction for Reasoning Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Value-aware stochastic KV cache eviction method improves reasoning model accuracy under compression by protecting large-magnitude states and promoting diverse eviction decisions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reasoning models improve accuracy through extended chains of thought, but their long outputs create a memory and compute bottleneck. KV cache eviction methods reduce this cost by evicting unimportant key-value pairs from the cache, yet they often yield worse accuracy than selection-based sparse attention alternatives, which keep the full KV cache. We identify key factors crucial to KV cache eviction accuracy. First, a small fraction of value states have abnormally large magnitudes, and evicting them causes catastrophic failure where models enter repetitive reasoning loops. Second, introducing stochasticity during eviction improves accuracy by increasing cache diversity. Based on these findings, we propose Value-aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction (VaSE), a training-free recipe that protects large-magnitude value states and promotes diverse eviction decisions. Across six reasoning tasks, Qwen3 models using VaSE with 4x KV cache compression yield higher average accuracies than SOTA selection method at the same sparsity, while outperforming the strongest eviction method by more than 4%. Overall, VaSE bridges the gap between efficiency and accuracy, supporting FlashAttention2 and enabling a static memory footprint for reasoning models.

ru.title: Защита важных состояний при сжатии памяти моделей рассуждений
ru.desc: Статья описывает метод Value-aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction (VaSE) для сжатия кэша ключ-значение в моделях рассуждений. Авторы выявили, что некоторые значения состояний имеют аномально большие магнитуды, и их удаление приводит к катастрофическому отказу модели с зацикливанием. Предложенный метод защищает эти критические значения и добавляет стохастичность при удалении кэша для повышения разнообразия. VaSE достигает сжатия KV кэша в 4 раза, превосходя существующие методы отбора и удаления на тестах по рассуждению, без необходимости переобучения.

en.title: Enhancing Model Accuracy with Smart Cache Eviction
en.desc: The paper introduces a new method called Value-aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction (VaSE) that enhances the accuracy of reasoning models while using less memory. It addresses the issue of cache eviction, where unimportant key-value pairs are removed to save resources, but often at the cost of model performance. The authors highlight that certain value states with large magnitudes are critical and should be preserved to avoid errors in reasoning. By incorporating randomness in the eviction process, VaSE promotes a more diverse selection of cache entries, leading to improved accuracy across various reasoning tasks compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 提升推理模型准确性的价值感知缓存驱逐方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种价值感知的随机KV缓存驱逐方法（VaSE），旨在提高压缩下推理模型的准确性。该方法通过保护大幅度状态，避免模型进入重复推理循环，从而减少了重要信息的丢失。VaSE还通过引入随机性来增加缓存的多样性，进一步提升了模型的表现。实验结果表明，使用VaSE的模型在多个推理任务中表现优于现有的选择性稀疏注意力方法。

papers.title: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6: Expanding the Frontier of Document Parsing with Under-Optimized Region Refinement and Progressive Post-Training
papers.abstract: Abstract PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 enhances document parsing performance through targeted data optimization and progressive post-training techniques, achieving state-of-the-art results on OmniDocBench v1.6.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce PaddleOCR-VL-1.6, an upgraded compact document parsing model built upon PaddleOCR-VL-1.5. Although PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 establishes a strong 0.9B baseline, its remaining errors concentrate in under-optimized regions where model behavior is unstable, data coverage is sparse, or supervision is unreliable. Rather than expanding the training corpus indiscriminately, PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 introduces a region-aware data optimization framework that identifies weak regions from the previous model, applies targeted enhancement to these regions, and improves the reliability of supervision signals. It further adopts a progressive post-training recipe based on curated data selection and reinforcement learning, pushing model performance to a higher level through staged optimization. PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 achieves a new state-of-the-art score of 96.33% on OmniDocBench v1.6, demonstrates strong competitiveness against top-tier VLMs, and provides a practical post-training recipe for the PaddleOCR-VL series.

ru.title: Умная оптимизация слабых точек для идеального распознавания документов
ru.desc: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 — это улучшенная модель для распознавания и парсинга документов, которая строится на основе предыдущей версии 1.5. Ключевая инновация заключается в применении целевой оптимизации данных: система анализирует области, где модель показывает нестабильное поведение, и сосредотачивает усилия на улучшении именно этих участков вместо простого расширения набора данных. Модель использует прогрессивное постобучение с применением техник выбора данных и обучения с подкреплением для пошагового повышения качества. В результате PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 достигает новый рекордный результат 96.33% на бенчмарке OmniDocBench v1.6 и конкурирует с лучшими моделями зрения-языка.

en.title: Targeted Optimization for Superior Document Parsing
en.desc: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 is an advanced document parsing model that improves upon its predecessor, PaddleOCR-VL-1.5, by focusing on specific areas where the previous model struggled. It uses a region-aware data optimization framework to enhance performance in under-optimized regions, ensuring better data coverage and more reliable supervision. Additionally, the model employs a progressive post-training approach that incorporates curated data selection and reinforcement learning to refine its capabilities further. As a result, PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 achieves a remarkable accuracy of 96.33% on the OmniDocBench v1.6 benchmark, setting a new standard in document parsing performance.

zh.title: 文档解析的新高度：PaddleOCR-VL-1.6
zh.desc: PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 是一个升级版的文档解析模型，旨在通过针对性的数据优化和渐进式后训练技术来提升性能。该模型在 OmniDocBench v1.6 上达到了 96.33% 的新状态，显示出其在文档解析领域的竞争力。与之前的版本相比，PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 通过识别和增强模型表现不佳的区域，优化了数据覆盖和监督信号的可靠性。此外，它还采用了基于精选数据和强化学习的渐进式后训练方法，进一步提升了模型的整体表现。

papers.title: Diagnosing Harmful Continuation in Answer-Correct Long-CoT Training Traces
papers.abstract: Abstract Answer-correct long chain-of-thought traces can lead to different fine-tuning outcomes, with post-conclusion continuations identified as harmful to training, characterized by uncertainty-geometry mismatches and addressed through a lightweight boundary proxy method.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Long chain-of-thought (CoT) traces are widely used as supervision for reasoning-oriented LLM SFT, yet answer-correct traces can still lead to markedly different fine-tuning outcomes. We study post-conclusion continuation in answer-correct long-CoT data: a continuation where the answer appears sufficiently supported, but the trace continues with additional reasoning that remains in the supervised target. To test its training effect, we use a delete-only editor to construct answer-preserving suffix removal and compare CoT-based SFT on the original and processed traces. We observe improved SFT outcomes after removing the editor-identified post-conclusion continuation, suggesting that this continuation is harmful to training in our setting. We therefore refer to this empirically supported phenomenon as harmful continuation. Beyond this intervention, we further characterize the removed post-conclusion continuation through uncertainty and hidden-state progress. We observe persistent local uncertainty together with weakened terminal-directional progress, forming an uncertainty--geometry mismatch. Finally, we instantiate Harmful Continuation Cut (HCC), a lightweight boundary proxy that approximates the editor-identified post-conclusion continuation boundary.

ru.title: Обрезание вредоносных продолжений рассуждений для оптимального обучения LLM
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема вредоносного продолжения рассуждений в длинных цепочках рассуждений (CoT), которые используются для fine-tuning LLM. Авторы обнаружили, что даже когда ответ уже полностью обоснован, продолжение трассировки рассуждений негативно влияет на качество обучения, что характеризуется несоответствием между неопределённостью модели и прогрессом в скрытом пространстве. Они предложили метод Harmful Continuation Cut (HCC) — лёгкий прокси-инструмент для автоматического определения границы между необходимыми и вредоносными частями рассуждений. Эта работа демонстрирует, что селективное удаление избыточных рассуждений из обучающих данных значительно улучшает результаты SFT для моделей, ориентированных на логические выводы.

en.title: Cutting Harmful Continuations for Better Model Training
en.desc: This paper investigates the impact of long chain-of-thought (CoT) traces on fine-tuning outcomes in large language models (LLMs). It identifies a phenomenon called 'harmful continuation', where additional reasoning after a correct answer negatively affects training. The authors propose a method to remove these harmful continuations, leading to improved supervised fine-tuning (SFT) results. They also introduce the Harmful Continuation Cut (HCC), a lightweight proxy to help identify and mitigate these detrimental continuations during training.

zh.title: 去除有害后续，提升模型微调效果
zh.desc: 本文研究了长链推理（CoT）数据中，正确答案的后续推理对模型微调的影响。尽管这些后续推理看似支持答案，但实际上会导致训练效果下降。我们通过删除后续推理的方式，发现去除这些有害的后续推理后，微调结果显著改善。最后，我们提出了一种轻量级的边界代理方法，称为有害后续切割（HCC），用于近似识别这些有害后续推理的边界。

papers.title: ClawHub Security Signals: When VirusTotal, Static Analysis, and SkillSpector Disagree
papers.abstract: Abstract Agent skills require layered security governance due to scanner disagreement, with findings showing varying detection rates across different scanner types and attack surfaces.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Agent skills extend AI agents with reusable instructions, tools, scripts, references, and workflows, establishing a security boundary distinct from both model safety and traditional package-malware detection. ClawHub Security Signals is a sanitized dataset of 67,453 latest public OpenClaw skill versions. Each row pairs redacted SKILL.md content and sanitized bundled files where present with a final ClawScan registry verdict and evidence from three scanner families: VirusTotal, static heuristic analysis, and NVIDIA SkillSpector.   Rather than estimating malicious-skill prevalence, we study scanner disagreement. The three scanners rarely flag the same skills: any pair overlaps on at most 10.4% of their combined positives, only 0.69% of skills are flagged by all three, and 81.9% of flagged skills are identified by a single scanner. The disagreement is structured by attack surface. SkillSpector, which raises semantic agentic-risk advisories rather than malware-reputation signals, is positive for 19,209 of 25,504 suspicious rows (75.3%) but only 14 of 206 malicious rows (6.8%). The malicious-verdict region shows the inverse profile: 150 of 206 malicious rows (72.8%) are VirusTotal-positive, consistent with bundled-code malware evidence.   These results show that agent-skill security requires layered governance, not single-scanner allow/block decisions. The corpus is released as a sanitized silver-standard dataset: labels are the registry's automated verdicts, not human-annotated ground truth, and the release represents an early, versioned snapshot intended to support the community while a human-annotated subset is developed. Further research is encouraged, including models tailored for skill-security triage.

ru.title: Многоуровневая система безопасности для AI агентов на основе консенсуса нескольких сканеров
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется проблема обнаружения вредоносных навыков для AI агентов с использованием трёх различных сканеров безопасности. Авторы создали датасет ClawHub Security Signals из 67,453 публичных версий навыков и проанализировали согласованность verdictов между VirusTotal, статическим анализом и NVIDIA SkillSpector. Исследование показало значительное несогласие между сканерами: только 0,69% навыков флагируются всеми тремя сканерами, а 81,9% обнаруженных угроз определяются только одним сканером. На основе полученных результатов авторы рекомендуют использовать многоуровневый подход к управлению безопасностью навыков вместо принятия решений на основе одного сканера.

en.title: Layered Security Governance for AI Agent Skills
en.desc: This paper discusses the need for layered security governance in AI agent skills due to inconsistencies in detection rates among different security scanners. It highlights the creation of a dataset called ClawHub Security Signals, which includes various versions of AI skills and their detection results from three different scanners. The study reveals significant disagreement among scanners, with only a small percentage of skills being flagged by all three, indicating that relying on a single scanner for security decisions is insufficient. The findings suggest that a more comprehensive approach is necessary for evaluating the security of AI skills, paving the way for future research in skill-security models.

zh.title: 代理技能安全治理需分层策略
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了代理技能的安全治理问题，指出不同扫描器在检测率上存在显著差异。研究表明，三种扫描器之间的重叠率极低，只有0.69%的技能被所有三种扫描器标记为恶意。为了应对这种扫描器之间的不一致性，作者建议采用分层的安全治理策略，而不是单一的允许或阻止决策。最后，研究提供了一个经过清洗的银标准数据集，以支持社区的进一步研究。

papers.title: Pressure-Testing Deception Probes in LLMs: Scaling, Robustness, and the Geometry of Deceptive Representations
papers.abstract: Abstract Linear probes for deception detection in large language models fail under distributional shifts despite high performance on clean data, revealing that deception is encoded through distributed sub-threshold features rather than simple linear directions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Linear probes trained on LLM activations are increasingly proposed as deception-detection metrics, yet report AUROC exceeding 0.96 on clean benchmarks while collapsing under distributional shift. This paper systematically pressure-tests probe-based metrics across the Gemma 3 model family (1B-27B parameters), diagnosing why they fail rather than merely documenting that they fail. We test four hypotheses about deception encoding: (1) single linear direction, (2) multi-dimensional subspace, (3) convex conic hull, (4) entropy proxy. Our design includes cross-domain transfer matrices, multi-dimensional probe analysis with permutation null baselines, entropy-residualization tests, and distractor evaluations across 8 stylistic shifts. We find that: (a) probes achieve near-perfect AUROC (>=0.998) on clean data but collapse under stylistic shifts; style-augmented probes recover near-perfect detection (mean AUROC 0.979-0.983) on unseen styles; (b) the single-direction hypothesis is rejected (k=1 captures only 0.61-0.80 AUROC), with cross-domain transfer failure confirmed as geometric rather than layer-mismatch-driven; (c) the entropy-proxy hypothesis is rejected (max |rho|=0.454, max Delta-AUROC after residualization=0.004); and (d) deception does not form a significant linear subspace (per-domain k*=0), yet multi-dimensional probes (k>=5) recover the signal through distributed sub-threshold features. Probe fragility reflects distributional narrowness rather than an architectural limitation: style-augmented probes recover near-perfect detection at both 4B and 27B, establishing that the inverse scaling pattern is a training-distribution artifact rather than a genuine scale-dependent phenomenon.

ru.title: Обман скрывается в деталях: почему линейные зонды не видят распределённые признаки лжи
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема детектирования обмана в больших языковых моделях с использованием линейных зондов (linear probes). Авторы обнаруживают, что зонды достигают высокой точности на чистых данных (AUROC > 0.96), но полностью теряют эффективность при распределительных сдвигах. Через серию экспериментов установлено, что обман кодируется не в простых линейных направлениях активаций, а через распределённые многомерные признаки, требующие по крайней мере пяти размерностей для восстановления сигнала. Расширение тренировочных данных стилистическими вариациями позволяет восстановить стабильное детектирование (AUROC 0.979-0.983) на невидимых стилях и показывает, что проблема заключается в узости распределения данных, а не в архитектурном ограничении модели.

en.title: Unveiling Deception: Beyond Linear Probes in Language Models
en.desc: This paper investigates the effectiveness of linear probes for detecting deception in large language models (LLMs) and reveals their limitations under different data distributions. Despite achieving high performance on clean datasets, these probes fail when faced with stylistic shifts, indicating that deception is represented through complex, distributed features rather than simple linear patterns. The authors conduct a series of tests to understand why these probes fail, rejecting several hypotheses about how deception is encoded. Ultimately, they find that multi-dimensional probes can successfully detect deception by leveraging sub-threshold features, highlighting the need for more robust detection methods in varying contexts.

zh.title: 揭示欺骗检测的多维特征
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了在大型语言模型中使用线性探针进行欺骗检测的有效性。尽管在干净数据上表现良好，但在分布变化时，线性探针的性能显著下降。研究表明，欺骗信息并不是通过简单的线性方向编码，而是通过分布式的亚阈值特征来表示。通过多维探针分析和交叉领域转移矩阵，作者发现多维探针能够更好地恢复欺骗信号。
